title: Chopper ProgressEvent v1
description: Wire-format schema for a single ProgressSink event, serialized as one JSON object per line (JSONL) on stderr when a machine-output transport is negotiated. v1 does NOT ship a stderr JSONL mode — that is deferred to FD-10 — but the on-wire shape is frozen here so tests can round-trip and so a future GUI can consume without re-specifying. Each event corresponds to one ProgressSink method call: phase_started / phase_done / step. Authoritative method signatures live in technical_docs/ARCHITECTURE_PLAN.md §5.

schema.description: Schema identifier. Mismatched consumers refuse to parse.

run_id.description: Same run_id as the RunResult for this invocation. Lets consumers multiplex events from multiple runs.

event.description: Which ProgressSink method produced this event.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (with Z suffix) of when the event was emitted.

phase.description: Current pipeline phase. For step events, the phase is the one whose `phase_started` was most recently emitted.

phase_index.description: Numeric index of `phase` (0..7) for ordinal progress bars.

phase_total.description: Total phases in the pipeline. Fixed at 8 for v1 (P0..P7). Present on phase_started/phase_done for bar initialization.

step_index.description: 0-indexed step within the current phase, when the service knows the count. Null for phases that don't enumerate steps.

step_total.description: Total steps in this phase, when known in advance. Null when the service can't estimate (e.g., streaming parse).

message.description: Short human-readable label, e.g. 'Parsing procs in utils.tcl'. No newlines.

detail.description: Free-form structured detail for GUIs. Consumers must tolerate unknown keys.

elapsed_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds elapsed since phase_started. Present on phase_done and step events.
